page: Microsoft Azure E8d_v5 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the EDv5 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8370C x86_64 processor operating at 2.8 GHz, configured with 8 dedicated vCPUs derived from 4 physical cores with hyper-threading. The processor includes 48 KB L1d cache, 1 MB L2 cache, and 48 MB L3 cache. It is equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory, providing an 8.0 GB per core ratio, and includes 307 GB of local NVMe storage. No GPU is present.

Benchmark data positions the E8d_v5 within the average performance tier for most compute and memory operations. It achieves a single-core stress-ng score of 1898.48 and a multi-core score of 7760.02. Memory bandwidth tests show 415.87 GB/sec for cached small-block reads and 56.69 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads. However, multi-threaded bzip3 decompression is relatively weak at 29.07 GB/sec, and LLM inference on a medium 7B model is limited, yielding 17.56 tokens/sec for prompt processing and 7.71 tokens/sec for text generation.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the E8d_v5 offers a high memory-to-vCPU density combined with local storage, making it a viable option for workloads requiring substantial RAM without the overhead of high vCPU counts. The inclusion of 307 GB of local storage reduces the need for external storage attachments for temporary data. This profile is suited for memory-intensive applications, relational databases, caching layers such as Redis, and moderate data processing tasks.
description: Microsoft Azure E8d_v5 is an x86_64 memory-optimized virtual machine powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor running at 2.8 GHz. It features 8 dedicated vCPUs, 64.0 GB of RAM, and 307 GB of local storage, with no GPU. Operating on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, the instance delivers average performance across most benchmarks, including a Geekbench multi-core score of 5872.0 and a Redis SET throughput of 7.75 million operations per second. However, it exhibits weaker performance in multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and medium-sized LLM inference tasks. Qualitatively, the E8d_v5 provides high memory density and integrated local storage, making it cost-effective for memory-bound workloads. It is best suited for relational databases, caching layers, and general memory-intensive enterprise applications.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E8d_v5 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 8 vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and 307 GB local storage, delivering balanced performance for database and caching workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E8d_v5 is an 8-vCPU memory-optimized virtual machine with 64 GB RAM and 307 GB local storage, running on Intel Xeon processors.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring high RAM-to-vCPU density and local storage for database and caching workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8370C processor with 8 dedicated vCPUs and 64 GB RAM
Includes 307 GB of local storage for fast temporary data access
Delivers 7.75 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks
Weaker performance in multi-threaded decompression and medium LLM inference
Suited for relational databases, caching layers, and memory-intensive workloads
